Assignment - Week 07

Yara Roth

2026-04-17

Setup

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(motifmatchr)
  library(MotifDb)
  library(universalmotif)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(sechm)
  library(limma)
  library(betterChromVAR)
  library(epiwraps)
  library(AnnotationHub)
  library(memes)
  library(ensembldb)
  library(magick)
  library(BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)
})
## Warning: package 'S4Vectors' was built under R version 4.5.3
## See system.file("LICENSE", package="MotifDb") for use restrictions.
library(rstudioapi)
setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
dir.create("data")
## Warning in dir.create("data"): 'data' already exists

Part I

To look at the accessability of the SP1 TF motif we will do the following: 1. Generate NF and mono-nucleosome tracks from ATAC-seq data
2. Retrieve TF motif
3. Find motif sites in genome
4. Look at ATAC signals around the motif occurences

Downloading ATAC-seq data from Chr19

download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/w7/atac.chr19.bam", "data/atac.chr19.bam", mode="wb")
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/w7/atac.chr19.bam.bai", "data/atac.chr19.bam.bai", mode="wb")

Preparing tracks

bam <- "data/atac.chr19.bam"

# create track to look at insertion profiles of nucleosome-free fragment
epiwraps::bam2bw(bam, output_bw = "data/NF_cuts.bw", paired=TRUE, binWidth=1L, type="ends", minFragLength=30, 
       maxFragLength=120, trim=4L, extend=3L, forceSeqlevelsStyle = "Ensembl")
## Reading in signal...
## Writing bigwig...
# create track using the centers of mono-nucleosome fragments
bam2bw(bam, output_bw = "data/mono_centers.bw", paired=TRUE, binWidth=5L, minFragLength=140, trim=4L,
       maxFragLength=220, type="center", extend=10L, forceSeqlevelsStyle = "Ensembl")
## Reading in signal...
## Writing bigwig...

Obtaining sites with a SP1 or CTCF motif

I chose to make the analysis for both CTCF and SP1, which allows to compare the footprints of both.

# we will first start by retrieving the SP1 TF motifs from the MotifDb database

motif_sp1 <- MotifDb::query(MotifDb, c("SP1","Mus"))[[1]]
motif2_sp1 <- convert_motifs(motif_sp1, class="TFBSTools-PFMatrix")
## Note: motif [motif] has an empty nsites slot, using 100.
motif_ctcf <- MotifDb::query(MotifDb, c("CTCF","Mus"))[[1]]
motif2_ctcf <- convert_motifs(motif_ctcf, class="TFBSTools-PFMatrix")
## Note: motif [motif] has an empty nsites slot, using 100.
# retrieving latest mouse genome
ah <- AnnotationHub()

query_results <- AnnotationHub::query(ah, c("Mus Musculus","TwoBitFile","101","GRCm38","primary_assembly"))
head(query_results)
## AnnotationHub with 3 records
## # snapshotDate(): 2025-10-29
## # $dataprovider: Ensembl
## # $species: mus musculus
## # $rdataclass: TwoBitFile
## # additional mcols(): taxonomyid, genome, description,
## #   coordinate_1_based, maintainer, rdatadateadded, preparerclass, tags,
## #   rdatapath, sourceurl, sourcetype 
## # retrieve records with, e.g., 'object[["AH84785"]]' 
## 
##             title                                           
##   AH84785 | Mus_musculus.GRCm38.dna.primary_assembly.2bit   
##   AH84786 | Mus_musculus.GRCm38.dna_rm.primary_assembly.2bit
##   AH84787 | Mus_musculus.GRCm38.dna_sm.primary_assembly.2bit
genome_m <- ah[["AH84785"]]
## loading from cache
chr19 <- import(genome_m)["19"]
# find SP1 motif matches across Chr19
moi_sp1 <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motif2_sp1, chr19, out="positions", p.cutoff=1e-5)[[1]]
moi_sp1 <- as(setNames(moi_sp1,names(chr19)), "GRanges")

moi_ctcf <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motif2_ctcf, chr19, out="positions", p.cutoff=1e-5)[[1]]
moi_ctcf <- as(setNames(moi_ctcf,names(chr19)), "GRanges")

Plotting ATAC-signals around motifs

ATAC signals for CTCF motifs

tracks <- list.files("data/", pattern="bw$", full.names=TRUE)
names(tracks) <- gsub("\\.bw","",basename(tracks))

sm_ctcf <- signal2Matrix(tracks, moi_ctcf, w=5, extend=300)
## Reading data//mono_centers.bw
## Reading data//NF_cuts.bw
plotEnrichedHeatmaps(sm_ctcf, trim=0.95, multiScale = TRUE, axis_name = "0")

The signals of the nucleosome-free fragments shows a very low coverage in the very center and two large peaks adjacent. The low signal in the center corresponds to the region where the TF binds (prevents TN5 insertion) = TF footprint. The two adjecent peaks indicate that the chromatin is accessbile there.
When looking at the signals of the mono-nucleosome fragments, we see two peaks adjecent to the motifs center. This corresponds to the nucleosomes that get pushed to the side, when the TF binds.

ATAC signals around SP1 signals

tracks <- list.files("data/", pattern="bw$", full.names=TRUE)
names(tracks) <- gsub("\\.bw","",basename(tracks))

sm_sp1 <- signal2Matrix(tracks, moi_sp1, w=5, extend=300)
## Reading data//mono_centers.bw
## Reading data//NF_cuts.bw
plotEnrichedHeatmaps(sm_sp1, trim=0.95, multiScale = TRUE, axis_name = "0")

The signals of the nucleosome-free fragments show low coverage in the center, probably corresponding to the SP1 binding site. Interestingly, the signal for the nucleosome-free fragments shows a gradual, continuous increase towards the side (unlike CTCF, which displayed two sharp peaks adjecent to the center). For the mono-nucleosome fragments, we also see a rather gradual, continuous increase outwards. This indicates that SP1 has a weaker effect on nucleosome positioning and that the TF has a less pronounced footprint (compared to CTCF).

Part 2

To identify motifs that are differentially acessible in between males and females in stressed vs control we do the following:
1. Annotate peaks with GC bias
2. Get a list of motifs
3. Scan the ATAC seq peaks for motif matches
4. Calculate the motif accessability deviations
5. Perform differential analysis

Downloading ATAC data from stressed brain

I decided to work with the whole data (stressed and controlled).

download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/mouse_mm38_hippocampus.peakCounts.SE.rds", "data/peakCounts.rds", mode="wb")
se <- readRDS("data/peakCounts.rds")

Annotate regions

se <- GenomeInfoDb::keepStandardChromosomes(se, pruning.mode = "coarse")
seqlevelsStyle(se) <- "UCSC"
se <- addGCBias(se, genome=BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)

Motif matches

I will use the motif list from MotifDb.

motifs <- MotifDb::query(MotifDb, "HOCOMOCOv13")

Then we convert the motifs that they get accepted from motifmatchr.

motifs <- do.call(TFBSTools::PWMatrixList, convert_motifs(motifs, class="TFBSTools-PWMatrix"))
names(motifs) <- make.unique(sapply(motifs, \(x) gsub("\\..+","",x@name)))

And now we will scan the ATAC peaks for motif matches.

matches <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motifs, se, genome=BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)
matches
## class: RangedSummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 20660 1358 
## metadata(0):
## assays(1): motifMatches
## rownames: NULL
## rowData names(1): bias
## colnames(1358): LEUTX FBXL19 ... ZSCA1 ZSCA4
## colData names(1): name

Get motif deviations

Now we will get the motif deviations, showing the realtive accessibility of each motif in each sample.

dev <- betterChromVAR(se, matches)
dev
## class: SummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 1358 12 
## metadata(0):
## assays(2): deviations z
## rownames(1358): LEUTX FBXL19 ... ZSCA1 ZSCA4
## rowData names(6): name N ... var.pval var.adjPval
## colnames(12): Control_female_GSM6041841.bam
##   Control_female_GSM6041842.bam ... FSS_male_GSM6041857.bam
##   FSS_male_GSM6041858.bam
## colData names(1): depth

Differential Analysis

As i used the complete dataset (containing both stressed and control) we should ideally account for condition as a covariate.

dev$condition <- ifelse(grepl("FSS", colnames(dev)), "Stressed", "Control")
dev$sex <- ifelse(grepl("female", colnames(dev)), "female", "male")
dev
## class: SummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 1358 12 
## metadata(0):
## assays(2): deviations z
## rownames(1358): LEUTX FBXL19 ... ZSCA1 ZSCA4
## rowData names(6): name N ... var.pval var.adjPval
## colnames(12): Control_female_GSM6041841.bam
##   Control_female_GSM6041842.bam ... FSS_male_GSM6041857.bam
##   FSS_male_GSM6041858.bam
## colData names(3): depth condition sex
# fit model with sex as main effect and condition as covariate
design <- model.matrix(~sex + condition, data=as.data.frame(colData(dev)))

fit <- eBayes(lmFit(assay(dev, "z"), design))
res <- as.data.frame(topTable(fit, coef=2, number = Inf))
res$motif <- row.names(res)
ggplot(res, aes(logFC, -log10(adj.P.Val), label=motif)) + geom_text()

On the following heatmap we see the motifs that show the largest differences regarding their accessability in males compared to females.

sechm(dev, head(row.names(res)), gaps_at="sex", assayName = "z")
